b:T2f349,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>The image shows human red blood cells.</p>
<p style="text-align:center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align:center;">[Source: someoneice/123rf.com.]</p>
<p style="text-align:left;"> </p>
<p style="text-align:left;">Outline what will happen to human red blood cells if transferred to distilled water.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Stem cells can be used to treat Stargardt’s disease. State <strong>one</strong> other condition treated using stem cells.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Explain the propagation of nerve impulses along the membrane of a neuron.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>c:T42c,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>cells absorb water by <span style="text-decoration:underline;">osmosis</span> and swell/increase in volume<br/><strong>OR</strong><br/>cells burst/lyse;</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>leukemia/other diseases of the hematopoietic system / skin burns;</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. depolarization of part of axon/membrane triggers/causes depolarization of next part;<br/>b. local currents;<br/>c. diffusion of sodium ions between depolarized part and next/polarized part (of axon);<br/>d. resting potential reduced/polarization of membrane becomes less /change from -70 to -50mV;<br/>e. sodium channels open when -50mV/threshold potential reached;<br/>f. entry of sodium ions causes depolarization;<br/>g. saltatory conduction in myelinated neurons/axons;</p>
<p><em>Allow answers in an annotated diagram</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>d:T574,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>This was mostly well answered with candidates realising that water entry due to osmosis would cause the cells to burst.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>A range of conditions were suggested, most of which are not currently treatable using stem cells. Diabetes and neurological diseases were not accepted. 60 % of candidates gave an acceptable answer, with leukaemia much the commonest. The correlation between the mark on this question and overall scores was fairly low, which may suggest that inappropriate examples have been learned in some schools. It is important not to raise hopes of stem cell treatment for specific diseases when research is still continuing and regulatory approval is unlikely for many years.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>This was a more difficult question with a much higher correlation coefficient. Many candidates did not understand what is meant by propagation of an impulse along the membrane of a neuron and answers tended to include accounts of the whole sequence of events in an action potential or in synaptic transmission. The mark most frequently awarded was for the mechanism of saltatory conduction.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/6/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","6"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"21N.2.HL.TZ0.3"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"HL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","2"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"21N"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","1-4-membrane-transport, 1-1-introduction-to-cells, 6-5-neurons-and-synapses"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$c"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$d"}}]]}]]}]
